description: Search for Jira issues using JQL (Jira Query Language)

jql.description: A JQL query string to filter issues. Examples: 'project = WEBAPP AND status = Open', 'assignee = currentUser() AND resolution = Unresolved ORDER BY priority DESC'

maxResults.description: The maximum number of items to return per page. To manage page size, Jira may return fewer items per page where a large number of fields are requested.

startAt.description: The index of the first item to return in a page of results (page offset).

fields.description: A list of fields to return for each issue. Use it to retrieve a subset of fields. Allowed values: *all (all fields), *navigable (all navigable fields), summary (just the summary field), -description (all navigable fields except description). Examples: summary,comment, -description, *all,-comment.

expand.description: Use expand to include additional information about issues in the response. Note that, unlike the majority of instances where a named entity is used, expand is specified as a comma-separated string of values. Expand options include: renderedFields, names, schema, operations, editmeta, changelog, versionedRepresentations.

properties.description: A list of issue property keys for issue properties to include in the results.

fieldsByKeys.description: Reference fields by their key (rather than ID). The default is false.
